rescue_reason: CatBoost GPU (and CPU) mode failed with TypeError on None values in categorical columns
failure_detail: TypeError: must be real number, not NoneType - 92 all-null object-dtype columns in official_user_features_full.parquet misidentified as categorical by fit_catboost(). Root cause: ROLLING_WINDOWS with empty time windows produce all-None object-dtype columns after left-merge; features.py numeric_fill_zero only fills already-numeric dtype columns.

feature_parquet_dtype_fix.description: Cast 92 all-null object-dtype windowed feature columns to float64 in official_user_features_full.parquet before train stage. These columns represent numeric aggregations (count/sum/avg/max) over empty time windows and contain no information regardless of dtype. Casting to float64 prevents CatBoost from treating them as categorical features.
feature_parquet_dtype_fix.method: df[col].astype('float64') for each all-null object column
feature_parquet_dtype_fix.semantic_impact: None - all-null columns provide zero information in either float64 or object dtype representation

command: PYTHONPATH=. python -m official.pipeline (features stage cached; train stage onward)
